ocr.text: In the evolving landscape of the food and beverage industry, companies like PepsiCo are increasingly prioritizing sustainability
metrics, particularly regarding packaging and plant-based product adoption.

The data presented in the accompanying tables illustrate the company's strategic focus on reducing environmental impact while
enhancing distribution network efficiency. Notably, the total spend on packaging sustainability initiatives reached USD($) 52M,
with a significant portion, USD($) 14M, allocated to glass materials, reflecting a commitment to eco-friendly practices.
Furthermore, the WasteReduction segment highlights a total spend of USD($) 39M, emphasizing efforts to minimize waste in
operations. In addition to packaging, the report outlines the robust growth trajectory of plant-based snacks, where the sales
growth for Plant-BasedSnacks is projected to be substantial, contributing to an overall market share of 30%.

The plant-based beverages segment also shows promise, with a market share of 25% and an emphasis on maintaining a sales
growth rate of Plant-BasedBeverages.

Coupled with a distribution network that effectively manages an average volume of 5.0M across 100 branches, PepsiCo is
strategically positioned to lead in both sustainability and product innovation in the North American market.
